title: phoradendron juniperinum subsp. libocedri
intro: Phoradendron juniperinum subsp. libocedri, also known as the mistletoe of the incense cedar, is a subspecies of parasitic plant that grows on the branches of its host tree, obtaining nutrients by tapping into the vascular system of the host plant. Traditionally, the leaves of Phoradendron juniperinum subsp. libocedri have been used in herbal medicine, and they are valued for their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which are said to offer relief from respiratory issues and skin conditions. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating coughs, and treating infections, as well as promoting wound healing and soothing irritated skin. Phoradendron juniperinum subsp. libocedri can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
